title: Cross version query error: unable to find settings issue
description:
### Company or project name
Cross cluster architecture
### Question
The current architecture is clickhouse 25.3, which creates and queries local tables of clickhouse 22.8 remotely through distributed, clickhouse25.3（Distributed table）-> clickhouse22.8（local table）.
The clickhouse 25.3 version creates the following table:
> create table test.ck25_dis on clsuter ck25_cluster
(uid Int32,
uname String
)engine=Distributed('ck22_cluster','test','ck22_local',rand());
The clickhouse 22.8 version creates tables as follows:
> create table test.ck22_localon clsuter ck22_cluster
(uid Int32,
uname String
)engine=ReplicatedMergeTree() order by uid;
The execution statement is as follows.（in clickhouse25.3）
1. Insert statement
> insert into test.ck25_dis  values(1,'a');
2.query statement
> select uid,uname from test.ck25_dis ;
The results showed that both the query and insertion reported errors. The specific information is as follows
> DB:Exception：Unknow setting allow_experiment_analyzer（UNKNOW SETTINGS）
Is there any way to solve the problem of querying low versions across clusters for high versions
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test.ck25_dis (uid INT, uname VARCHAR(255));
CREATE TABLE test.ck22_local (uid INT, uname VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test.ck25_dis (uid, uname) VALUES (2, NULL);
SELECT uid, uname FROM test.ck25_dis WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM test.ck22_local WHERE uname IS NOT NULL);
key_bug_features: Cross version query with unknown settings error
